page.content: The analysis presented in the accompanying table focuses on the relationship between various clinical metrics and their impact on health outcomes, particularly in the context of protein excretion levels and renal function. Key metrics such as Log PCR (Protein-to-Creatinine Ratio) and Log ACR (Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio) are crucial in assessing renal health and predicting potential complications in patients. The findings indicate a statistically significant association between elevated Log PCR values and increased health risks, as evidenced by a hazard ratio (HR) of 1.39 (95% CI: 1.18–1.62), suggesting that higher protein excretion is linked to adverse outcomes. Furthermore, the Log 24-h protein excretion metric provides critical insights into the extent of protein loss within a 24-hour period, reinforcing the importance of monitoring these parameters in clinical practice. The Beta coefficients and their corresponding standard errors highlight the robustness of these relationships, underscoring the need for further investigation into the underlying mechanisms that contribute to these associations. Overall, the data underscore the importance of renal function metrics in predicting patient prognosis.

on health outcomes, particularly in the context of protein excretion levels and renal function. Key metrics such as Log PCR
(Protein-to-Creatinine Ratio) and Log ACR (Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio) are crucial in assessing renal health and predicting
potential complications in patients. The findings indicate a statistically significant association between elevated Log PCR values
and increased health risks, as evidenced by a hazard ratio (HR) of 1.39 (95% CI: 1.18–1.62), suggesting that higher protein
excretion is linked to adverse outcomes. Furthermore, the Log 24-h protein excretion metric provides critical insights into the
extent of protein loss within a 24-hour period, reinforcing the importance of monitoring these parameters in clinical practice.
The Beta coefficients and their corresponding standard errors highlight the robustness of these relationships, underscoring the
need for further investigation into the underlying mechanisms that contribute to these associations. Overall, the data underscore
the importance of renal function metrics in predicting patient prognosis.
